triage_rationale.age_factor: Risk of cardiac causes increases significantly with age, especially over 45 in men and 55 in women.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Women may present with atypical MI symptoms: jaw pain, fatigue, nausea rather than classic chest pressure.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Chest pain in pregnancy may indicate peripartum cardiomyopathy or pulmonary embolism; requires urgent evaluation.

Chest pain with shortness of breath and diaphoresis
Sudden tearing chest pain radiating to the back (aortic dissection)
Chest pain with syncope or near-syncope
Chest pain with low blood pressure or rapid heart rate

Chest pain related to acid reflux not controlled by antacids
Chest pain with reproducible tenderness on palpation (costochondritis)
green_home_care: Chest wall pain reproducible by pressing on ribs
Mild heartburn improving with antacids
Chest pain clearly related to anxiety with no other symptoms

llm_analysis_tips: Always rule out ACS first before considering benign causes of chest pain.
Ask about risk factors: smoking, diabetes, hypertension, family history.
A 12-lead ECG and troponin level are essential initial workup.
Consider pulmonary embolism if there is leg swelling or recent immobility.

symptoms.label: Cough

why_assess_carefully: Coughing up blood may indicate tuberculosis, lung cancer, or pulmonary embolism.
Acute onset cough with fever may indicate pneumonia or bronchitis.
Chronic cough lasting more than 8 weeks warrants investigation for underlying cause.
Cough with severe shortness of breath may indicate acute asthma or heart failure.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Children with barking cough should be evaluated for croup; adults over 50 with new cough need malignancy screening.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: ACE inhibitor-induced cough is more common in women.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Persistent cough in pregnancy can cause urinary incontinence; treat underlying cause safely.

immediate_red_flags: Coughing up significant amounts of blood (hemoptysis)
Cough with severe difficulty breathing or blue lips
Sudden onset cough with choking (possible foreign body aspiration)
Cough with high fever and productive yellow-green sputum
Cough with unexplained significant weight loss

Cough with severe respiratory distress and blue lips
Sudden cough with choking and inability to speak
yellow_doctor_visit: Cough with fever and productive sputum lasting more than 3 days
Chronic cough lasting more than 2-3 weeks
Cough with unexplained weight loss
Cough with wheezing not controlled by inhaler
green_home_care: Mild dry cough with clear runny nose typical of viral upper respiratory infection
Post-viral cough lasting less than 3 weeks with no other symptoms
Cough due to known seasonal allergies responding to antihistamines

llm_analysis_tips: Classify cough as acute (<3 weeks), subacute (3-8 weeks), or chronic (>8 weeks).
Chronic cough triad: postnasal drip, GERD, and asthma account for most cases.
Always ask about smoking history and occupational exposures.
ACE inhibitor use is a common reversible cause of dry cough.

advice.action: For mild viral cough, rest and hydrate. Seek care if cough produces blood or lasts more than 3 weeks.
home_care_if: Mild viral cough with runny nose and no fever
Known allergy cough controlled by antihistamines
advice.reason: Most acute coughs resolve on their own, but persistent or productive cough may indicate serious infection or malignancy.

symptoms.label: Fatigue

why_assess_carefully: Fatigue can be a symptom of serious conditions including anemia, heart failure, or malignancy.
Unexplained persistent fatigue may indicate thyroid disorders or diabetes.
Sudden severe fatigue with chest pain or shortness of breath requires emergency evaluation.
Chronic fatigue syndrome is a real and debilitating condition requiring proper diagnosis.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Fatigue in elderly patients may mask underlying cardiac or renal disease.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Women are more commonly affected by autoimmune causes of fatigue such as lupus and hypothyroidism.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Fatigue in early pregnancy is normal but severe fatigue may indicate anemia or gestational diabetes.

immediate_red_flags: Sudden extreme fatigue with chest pain or shortness of breath
Fatigue with confusion or altered consciousness
Fatigue with severe unintentional weight loss
Fatigue with high fever and severe body aches
Fatigue in a known diabetic with abnormal blood sugar

Fatigue with altered mental status
Fatigue with significant unintentional weight loss over short period
yellow_doctor_visit: Fatigue lasting more than 2 weeks without clear cause
Fatigue with weight change, hair loss, or temperature intolerance (thyroid)
Fatigue with recurrent infections or easy bruising
Fatigue in a patient with poorly controlled chronic disease
green_home_care: Fatigue following a viral illness improving gradually
Fatigue clearly related to poor sleep hygiene
Fatigue related to known physical or emotional overexertion

llm_analysis_tips: Distinguish between sleepiness (desire to sleep) and true fatigue (lack of energy).
Screen for depression, as it is one of the most common causes of fatigue.
Basic labs: CBC, thyroid panel, blood glucose, and metabolic panel.
Ask about sleep quality, stress, diet, and physical activity level.

advice.action: Rest and improve sleep hygiene for mild fatigue; seek medical evaluation if persistent or accompanied by other symptoms.
emergency_if: Sudden severe fatigue with chest pain or breathlessness
home_care_if: Post-viral fatigue improving gradually
Fatigue from overexertion without other symptoms
advice.reason: Fatigue is one of the most common medical complaints and has a wide range of causes from benign to serious.

symptoms.label: Sore Throat

why_assess_carefully: Streptococcal pharyngitis requires antibiotic treatment to prevent rheumatic fever.
Peritonsillar abscess can cause airway compromise and is a medical emergency.
Epiglottitis is a rapidly progressing life-threatening infection causing sore throat and drooling.
Throat pain may indicate Ludwig's angina or deep space neck infection.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Strep throat is most common in school-age children; epiglottitis risk is higher in adults.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: No significant sex-specific differences in sore throat presentation.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Sore throat in pregnancy should be treated promptly as severe infection can affect fetal wellbeing.

immediate_red_flags: Sore throat with drooling and inability to swallow (possible epiglottitis)
Throat swelling causing difficulty breathing or stridor
Severe unilateral throat pain with uvular deviation (peritonsillar abscess)
Sore throat with high fever and severe difficulty opening mouth (trismus)
Sore throat with muffled voice and neck swelling

Sore throat lasting more than 7 days
Sore throat with tender swollen lymph nodes and fatigue (possible mono)

Infectious mononucleosis can present similarly to strep and should be considered in young adults.
Avoid amoxicillin empirically if mono is suspected as it causes a rash.
Recurrent sore throat may warrant tonsillectomy referral.

advice.action: Treat viral sore throat with rest, fluids, and analgesics. Seek care if high fever, exudates, or difficulty swallowing.

doctor_visit_if: Fever with white spots on tonsils
Sore throat lasting more than 1 week
home_care_if: Mild sore throat with cold symptoms
Sore throat improving after 2-3 days
advice.reason: Most sore throats are viral and self-limiting, but bacterial infections require antibiotics.

symptoms.label: Urinary Symptoms

why_assess_carefully: Urinary symptoms may indicate UTI, kidney infection, or kidney stones.
Blood in urine can indicate bladder cancer or kidney disease.
Inability to urinate is a urological emergency.
Recurrent UTIs may indicate anatomical abnormalities or antibiotic resistance.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Older men with difficulty urinating should be evaluated for benign prostatic hyperplasia.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Women are significantly more prone to UTIs due to shorter urethra. Men with UTI need imaging to rule out structural abnormality.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: UTI during pregnancy requires prompt treatment as it can progress to pyelonephritis and preterm labor.

immediate_red_flags: Complete inability to urinate (urinary retention)
Blood in urine with fever and flank pain (pyelonephritis or kidney stone)
Urinary symptoms with altered mental status in the elderly (sepsis)
Urine output stopping completely in a known kidney disease patient
Severe suprapubic or flank pain with blood in urine

Urinary symptoms recurring for the second time within 3 months
Painless hematuria requiring urological workup

Mild irritation after catheter removal improving rapidly

llm_analysis_tips: Collect urinalysis and urine culture before starting antibiotics.
In men, consider prostatitis and STIs as causes of urinary symptoms.
Painless hematuria in a patient over 40 must be evaluated for malignancy.
Always check for pregnancy before prescribing antibiotics for UTI in women of childbearing age.

advice.action: Increase fluid intake, avoid irritants, and seek care for antibiotic treatment if UTI is suspected.
home_care_if: Mild urinary frequency without fever or pain in a healthy young adult
advice.reason: Urinary tract infections are common and treatable but untreated infections can spread to the kidneys.

symptoms.label: Joint Pain

why_assess_carefully: Septic arthritis is a medical emergency that can destroy a joint within hours.
Gout attacks cause severe pain and require prompt anti-inflammatory treatment.
Rheumatoid arthritis can lead to permanent joint deformity if untreated.
Joint pain may be the presenting feature of systemic inflammatory diseases.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Osteoarthritis is the most common cause in patients over 50. Reactive arthritis and gout are more common in younger adults.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Rheumatoid arthritis and lupus are more common in women. Gout is more common in men.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Joint pain in pregnancy may worsen due to hormonal ligament laxity; avoid NSAIDs especially in third trimester.

immediate_red_flags: Single hot, red, extremely tender swollen joint with fever (septic arthritis)
Joint pain following recent penetrating trauma
Sudden inability to bear weight on a joint
Joint pain with rash and fever (reactive arthritis or septic arthritis)
Migratory arthritis with cardiac symptoms (rheumatic fever)

Multiple joint pain with fatigue and rash
Joint pain not responding to OTC anti-inflammatories after 5 days

Mild joint stiffness improving with movement and warmth

llm_analysis_tips: Differentiate inflammatory (worse in morning, improves with activity) from mechanical (worse with activity, better with rest) joint pain.
Ask about recent infection or travel as reactive arthritis triggers.
Screen for uric acid levels if gout is suspected.
ANA, RF, and anti-CCP are key screening labs for autoimmune arthritis.

advice.reason: Joint pain has a broad spectrum of causes. Septic arthritis must be excluded promptly to preserve joint function.

symptoms.label: Swollen Lymph Nodes

why_assess_carefully: Swollen lymph nodes can indicate serious conditions including lymphoma or metastatic cancer.
Rapid enlargement of lymph nodes with systemic symptoms may indicate lymphoma.
Localized lymphadenopathy often reflects a regional infection.
Supraclavicular lymphadenopathy in adults almost always requires urgent investigation for malignancy.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Children commonly develop lymphadenopathy with viral infections. Adults over 40 with persistent swollen lymph nodes need malignancy evaluation.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Breast cancer can present with axillary lymph node enlargement; always examine in women.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Mild generalized lymphadenopathy can occur in normal pregnancy but persistent nodes require evaluation.

immediate_red_flags: Rapidly enlarging lymph node with fever and night sweats
Swollen lymph node that is very hard, fixed, or non-tender
Supraclavicular lymph node enlargement
Lymph node enlargement with unexplained weight loss

green_home_care: Tender cervical nodes during a known throat infection improving with antibiotics

The B-symptoms of lymphoma are: fever, drenching night sweats, and weight loss >10%.

Infectious mononucleosis and HIV are common causes of generalized lymphadenopathy.

symptoms.label: Anxiety

why_assess_carefully: Panic attacks can mimic cardiac or respiratory emergencies.
Severe anxiety can lead to suicidal ideation and self-harm.
Anxiety may be a symptom of an underlying medical condition such as hyperthyroidism.
Untreated chronic anxiety can lead to significant functional impairment.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Anxiety disorders often first appear in adolescence and young adulthood. Elderly patients may have anxiety masking depression.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Anxiety disorders are twice as common in women. Postpartum anxiety is underdiagnosed.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Anxiety is common in pregnancy; severe anxiety or panic disorder may require medication adjustment and mental health support.

immediate_red_flags: Anxiety with active suicidal or self-harm ideation
Panic attack mimicking cardiac event requiring medical evaluation to exclude
Acute psychosis or severe dissociation

Benzodiazepines are effective short-term but have addiction risk.

home_care_if: Mild situational anxiety improving with relaxation and breathing exercises

symptoms.label: Insomnia

why_assess_carefully: Chronic insomnia increases the risk of depression, anxiety, and cardiovascular disease.
Insomnia may be secondary to an underlying medical or psychiatric disorder.
Sleep deprivation impairs judgment and increases accident risk.
Obstructive sleep apnea commonly presents with insomnia and requires treatment.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Insomnia prevalence increases with age; older adults often have more fragmented sleep.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Women report higher rates of insomnia, especially during menopause and perimenopause.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Insomnia is common in pregnancy, especially in the third trimester; safe management strategies include sleep hygiene and CBT-I.

immediate_red_flags: Insomnia with suicidal ideation or severe psychiatric crisis
Insomnia with severe daytime impairment risking safety (e.g., driving)
Total inability to sleep for multiple days with hallucinations (rare, possible fatal familial insomnia)

home_care_if: Short-term insomnia related to stress

symptoms.label: Palpitations

Palpitations with syncope suggest a potentially fatal arrhythmia.
Palpitations can be benign (caffeine, anxiety) or serious (ventricular tachycardia).

triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Women are more prone to SVT (supraventricular tachycardia).
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Palpitations are common in pregnancy due to increased cardiac output; most are benign, but arrhythmias should be excluded.

Palpitations with severe chest pain
Palpitations with shortness of breath and low blood pressure
Palpitations in a patient with known heart disease lasting more than 30 minutes

triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Women are more prone to cyclical edema related to hormonal changes.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Ankle swelling is common in normal pregnancy, but sudden or severe swelling especially with headache and high blood pressure suggests pre-eclampsia.

symptoms.label: Loss of Appetite

Loss of appetite with significant weight loss warrants urgent investigation.
In elderly patients, anorexia is associated with increased mortality.
Depression and anxiety are common reversible causes of loss of appetite.

triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Anorexia nervosa is ten times more common in women than men; eating disorders require specialized care.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Nausea and loss of appetite in first trimester is usually normal; hyperemesis gravidarum requires treatment.

immediate_red_flags: Complete inability to eat or drink for more than 24-48 hours with dehydration
Loss of appetite with significant rapid unintentional weight loss
Loss of appetite in a known cancer patient with rapid deterioration
Anorexia with signs of malnutrition or electrolyte imbalance
Loss of appetite with severe abdominal pain or jaundice

symptoms.label: Jaundice

why_assess_carefully: Jaundice can indicate acute liver failure, biliary obstruction, or hemolysis.
Rapidly progressing jaundice with encephalopathy indicates acute liver failure.
Painless jaundice in an older patient is a classic presentation of pancreatic cancer.
Jaundice in neonates can cause kernicterus and brain damage if untreated.
triage_rationale.age_factor: Neonatal jaundice requires immediate phototherapy assessment. Painless jaundice in elderly raises cancer concern.
triage_rationale.sex_specific_notes: Primary biliary cholangitis is more common in women. Gallstones causing obstructive jaundice are more common in women.
triage_rationale.pregnancy_note: Intrahepatic cholestasis of pregnancy causes jaundice and itching and requires prompt management to prevent stillbirth.

symptoms.label: Numbness or Tingling

why_assess_carefully: Sudden numbness on one side of the body may indicate stroke.

symptoms.label: Nosebleed (Epistaxis)

symptoms.label: Unintentional Weight Loss

why_assess_carefully: Unintentional weight loss of more than 5% in 1 month or 10% in 6 months is clinically significant.
Malignancy is the most feared cause and must be excluded.
